name: module-health
description: Modular architecture health assessor for Logseq Template Graph. Analyzes module balance, cohesion, size distribution, and dependencies. Calculates health scores and suggests reorganization. Use when checking module structure, assessing architecture quality, or planning refactoring.
capabilities: Calculate health scores for each module (0-100 scale)
Identify bloated modules (50+ classes) and small modules (under 5)
Analyze class-to-property ratios and documentation coverage
Generate reorganization plans with effort estimates
Track module growth trends over time via git history
Map module dependencies and detect coupling issues
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This is a legitimate architecture analysis skill with low risk. All 107 static findings are false positives: external_commands findings are documentation examples or safe utilities (grep, bc, echo) for file analysis, and weak_crypto findings are from simplistic string matching on non-cryptographic content. The skill only reads source/ directory files and outputs analysis. No network calls, credential access, or dangerous execution patterns.
valueStatement: Large EDN templates become hard to maintain. This skill calculates health scores for each module based on balance, cohesion, and documentation. It identifies bloated modules, suggests reorganization plans, and tracks architecture quality over time.
limitations: Limited to Logseq Template Graph projects with source/ directory structure
Does not modify files or perform refactoring automatically
Health scores are guidelines, not absolute rules
Cannot analyze non-EDN projects or different module structures

useCases.title: Evaluate module organization
useCases.description: Run health checks before releases to ensure modules meet quality standards and identify refactoring needs

useCases.title: Plan reorganization
useCases.description: Get detailed analysis of misc/ module bloat with specific split strategies and time estimates

useCases.title: Track architecture quality
useCases.description: Monitor health scores over time to identify declining module quality and plan improvements

promptTemplates.title: Quick Health Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check module health and show me the overall health score, a summary table, and top issues

promptTemplates.title: Deep Module Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the misc/ module in detail and show me how to split it into focused modules

promptTemplates.title: Reorganization Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: How should I reorganize my modules? Show me a step-by-step plan with commands and effort estimates

promptTemplates.title: Track Changes Over Time
promptTemplates.prompt: How have my modules changed over the last 90 days? Show growth trends and health score history

bestPractices: Run health checks before major releases to catch architectural drift
Review health scores when adding new classes to existing modules
Use reorganization plans to break down large refactoring tasks
antiPatterns: Adding new classes to misc/ instead of creating focused modules
Ignoring health scores below 60 - they indicate real maintainability issues
Delaying reorganization - bloat compounds and becomes harder to fix

faq.question: Which projects work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Logseq Template Graph projects that have a source/ directory with modular EDN files.

faq.question: What does a health score measure?
faq.answer: Scores measure size balance, documentation quality, organization clarity, class-property ratio, and completeness (0-100 scale).

faq.question: Can the skill modify my files?
faq.answer: No. The skill analyzes and suggests but does not automatically refactor. You must approve and execute changes yourself.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads files in the source/ directory and outputs analysis. No data is sent anywhere.

faq.question: What if health score is low?
faq.answer: Low scores indicate issues like bloated modules or missing documentation. The skill provides specific recommendations and effort estimates to improve.

faq.question: How is this different from other code analyzers?
faq.answer: This is purpose-built for EDN template modular architecture, calculating scores specific to class-property organization patterns.
